case_name: Medstar Georgetown Medical Center, Inc. v. Kaplan

summary.brief: Medstar Georgetown Medical Center, Inc. v. Kaplan. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: A jury found MedStar Georgetown Medical Center, Inc. and MMG-GI at Lafayette Center d/b/a MedStar Medical Group II, LLC liable for breaching the national standard of care when treating David Kaplan
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: awarded him an aggregate of $4 million in damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: David Kaplan and for failing to obtain his informed consent for treatment
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr. Kaplan presented evidence at trial that he had suffered two distinct kinds of harm.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He experienced physical injury: the continuation of his severe Crohn’s disease that did not respond to the steroids MedStar prescribed, the complete deterioration of his hip bones, and the eventual replacement of both hips, which involved three hip surgeries with lengthy and painful recoveries.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: And he experienced emotional distress. Previously an “intense runn[er],” as a result of his hip replacements he suffered the loss of participating in an activity that he loved
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court correctly noted that “[t]he jury is afforded great weight in determining the appropriate measure of damages”
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The trial court also detailed the extensive testimony introduced at trial from Mr. Kaplan and his sister that shed light on “the impact [Mr. Kaplan’s] injuries had on his physical capabilities and the emotional turmoil he suffered.”
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, we cannot say that the trial court’s assessment that “the jury’s award was []reasonable in light of the evidence and testimony presented” constituted an abuse of discretion.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Severe Crohn's disease symptoms and hip pain
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Continuation of severe Crohn's disease not responding to steroids, deterioration of hip bones
medications: Steroids (specific type and dosage not documented in judgment)
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Severe Crohn's disease with hip deterioration

requestables.name: Gastroenterology Consult
requestables.description: Consultation for management of Crohn's disease

reveal.result_summary: Continuation of severe Crohn's disease not responding to steroids

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with severe Crohn's disease symptoms and hip pain

key_events.event: Patient reports severe Crohn's symptoms and hip pain

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Consultations and evaluations for Crohn's disease and hip deterioration

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with severe Crohn's disease not responding to steroids
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing the patient's Crohn's disease?

options.clinical_reasoning: Biologics are indicated when steroids are ineffective
Prolonged steroid use beyond recommended duration

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not transition to biologics

expected_action_court.description: Biologic therapy should have been initiated
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: National guidelines recommend biologics after steroid failure

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Prolonged steroid use led to complications
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Biologics should have been started to manage Crohn's disease
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Breach of national standard of care

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent three hip surgeries with lengthy and painful recoveries
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of ability to run and participate in activities

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to transition to biologics
Causation established - prolonged steroid use led to hip deterioration

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Biologics would have managed Crohn's disease effectively

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents with severe Crohn's disease symptoms and hip pain

court_findings.legal_significance: Breach of national standard of care
